title: phyllocladales
intro: Phyllocladales is an order of flowering plants characterized by the presence of phylloclades, which are flattened or leaf-like stems that perform photosynthesis, often found in plants that have reduced or absent true leaves. This order includes various families such as Orchidaceae, known for their diverse and often showy flowers, Euphorbiaceae, which comprises spurge and poinsettia, and Cyperaceae, a family of sedges and rushes. The phyllocladales order is primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. By adapting to their environments, plants within this order have evolved unique characteristics that enable them to thrive in a variety of ecosystems.

families.family_desc: Phyllonomaceae is a family of flowering plants in the order Ericales, native to the southeastern United States.
